steps.title: Graph both sides
steps.content: Graph the absolute-value expression and the horizontal line. The intersection points give the solutions to the equation.

steps.title: Find both solutions
steps.content:
The equation $|-5x+13|=73$ means the inside expression can equal $73$ or $-73$.
$$-5x+13=73$$
$$x=-12$$
$$-5x+13=-73$$
$$x=\frac{86}{5}$$

steps.title: Add the solutions
steps.content:
The question asks for the sum of the two solutions.
$$-12+\frac{86}{5}=\frac{-60+86}{5}=\frac{26}{5}$$
The answer is D.
